title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Section Test

questions.question: What is the remainder when the polynomial x¹⁰⁰ – 2x⁵¹ + 1 is divided by (x² – 1) ?
questions.explanation: ​Given:f(x)=x100−2x51+1Divisor=x2−1Concept Used:Remainder theorem for quadratic divisorFormula Used:If f(x) is divided by (x2−1),then remainder =ax+bwhere x2−1=0=&gtx=1,−1Solution:Let remainder =ax+bf(1)=a(1)+bf(−1)=a(−1)+bCompute f(1):f(1)=1100−2(1)51+1=1−2+1=0=&gta+b=0(1)Compute f(−1):f(−1)=(−1)100−2(−1)51+1=1−2(−1)+1=1+2+1=4=&gt−a+b=4(2)Solve (1) and (2):a+b=0−a+b=4Subtract (1) from (2):−2a=4a=−2b=2Remainder=ax+b=−2x+2Final Answer:−2x+2\textbf{Given:} \\f(x) = x^{100} - 2x^{51} + 1 \\\text{Divisor} = x^2 - 1 \\\textbf{Concept Used:} \\\text{Remainder theorem for quadratic divisor} \\\textbf{Formula Used:} \\\text{If } f(x) \text{ is divided by } (x^2-1), \\\text{then remainder } = ax + b \\\text{where } x^2-1 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 1,-1 \\\textbf{Solution:} \\\text{Let remainder } = ax + b \\f(1) = a(1) + b \\f(-1) = a(-1) + b \\\text{Compute } f(1): \\f(1) = 1^{100} - 2(1)^{51} + 1 \\= 1 - 2 + 1 \\= 0 \\\Rightarrow a + b = 0 \quad (1) \\\text{Compute } f(-1): \\f(-1) = (-1)^{100} - 2(-1)^{51} + 1 \\= 1 - 2(-1) + 1 \\= 1 + 2 + 1 \\= 4 \\\Rightarrow -a + b = 4 \quad (2) \\\text{Solve (1) and (2):} \\a + b = 0 \\-a + b = 4 \\\text{Subtract (1) from (2):} \\-2a = 4 \\a = -2 \\b = 2 \\\text{Remainder} = ax + b \\= -2x + 2 \\\textbf{Final Answer:} \\-2x + 2Given:f(x)=x100−2x51+1Divisor=x2−1Concept Used:Remainder theorem for quadratic divisorFormula Used:If f(x) is divided by (x2−1),then remainder =ax+bwhere x2−1=0=&gtx=1,−1Solution:Let remainder =ax+bf(1)=a(1)+bf(−1)=a(−1)+bCompute f(1):f(1)=1100−2(1)51+1=1−2+1=0=&gta+b=0(1)Compute f(−1):f(−1)=(−1)100−2(−1)51+1=1−2(−1)+1=1+2+1=4=&gt−a+b=4(2)Solve (1) and (2):a+b=0−a+b=4Subtract (1) from (2):−2a=4a=−2b=2Remainder=ax+b=−2x+2Final Answer:−2x+2​​

questions.question: Kanika travelled a distance of 54 Km in 10 hours. She travelled partly on foot at 3 Km/hr and partly by autorickshaw at 15 Km/hr. What is the distance travelled by autorickshaw?
questions.explanation: ​Given: Total Distance=54 Km,Total Time=10 hrs Sfoot=3,Sauto=15 Solution: Let distance by auto be D. Distance by foot is 54−D. Time=DistanceSpeed D15+54−D3=10 D+5(54−D)15=10 D+270−5D=150 −4D=−120 D=30 Km Final Answer 30 Km\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Total Distance} = 54 \text{ Km}, \text{Total Time} = 10 \text{ hrs} \\ \space \, \\ S_{foot} = 3, S_{auto} = 15 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Let distance by auto be } D. \text{ Distance by foot is } 54-D. \\ \space \, \\ \text{Time} = \frac{\text{Distance}}{\text{Speed}} \\ \space \, \\ \frac{D}{15} + \frac{54-D}{3} = 10 \\ \space \, \\ \frac{D + 5(54-D)}{15} = 10 \\ \space \, \\ D + 270 - 5D = 150 \\ \space \, \\ -4D = -120 \\ \space \, \\ D = 30 \text{ Km} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ 30 \text{ Km}Given: Total Distance=54 Km,Total Time=10 hrs Sfoot​=3,Sauto​=15 Solution: Let distance by auto be D. Distance by foot is 54−D. Time=SpeedDistance​ 15D​+354−D​=10 15D+5(54−D)​=10 D+270−5D=150 −4D=−120 D=30 Km Final Answer 30 Km​​

questions.question: From point C on the ground, the angle of elevation to the top of a cliff is 30°. From point D, which is 60m closer to the cliff, the angle of elevation is 60°. Find the ratio of the distance of C from the cliff's foot to the distance of D from the cliff's foot.
questions.explanation: Given: From point C, angle of elevation = 30° From point D, angle of elevation = 60° Point D is 60 m closer to the cliff than point C Find the ratio of distance of C to distance of D from the foot of the cliff Concept Used: Trigonometric ratio tan θ = (Height) / (Base) Formula Used: tan 30° = 1/√3 tan 60° = √3 tan⁡θ=HeightBase\tan\theta = \frac{\text{Height}}{\text{Base}}tanθ=BaseHeight​​​ Solution: Let distance of C from foot = x Then distance of D from foot = x − 60 Let height of cliff = h From point C: ​tan30°=hx ​​1√3=hx h=x√3tan 30° = \frac{h}{x}\\ \ \\ ​​\frac{1}{√3} =\frac{h}{x}\\ \ \\h = \frac{x}{√3}tan30°=xh​ ​​√31​=xh​ h=√3x​​​ From point D: tan 60° = h(x−60)\frac{h} { (x − 60)}(x−60)h​ ​​ √3 = h(x−60)\frac{h} { (x − 60)}(x−60)h​​​ h = √3 (x − 60) Equate heights: x√3\frac{x}{√3}√3x​​ = √3 (x − 60) Multiply both sides by √3: x = 3(x − 60) x = 3x − 180 2x = 180 x = 90 Distance of D = 90 − 60 = 30 Required ratio = 90 : 30 = 3 : 1 So the correct answer is (c).​​

questions.question: In the given figure, O is the centre of the circle. If the angles ∠AOB,∠OAC are, respectively, 140° and 50°, then the value of angle ∠BAC is ____ ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/cf0c4034-2d22-4e50-b6d0-0ad5f0345056.png)
questions.explanation: Given: O is the center of the circle. ∠AOB = 140° ∠OAC = 50° Concept Used: Angle subtended by an arc at the center is double the angle subtended by the same arc at any point on the remaining part of the circle. In a triangle, angles opposite to equal sides are equal. Sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. Solution: Consider triangle OAC. Since OA and OC are radii of the same circle, OA = OC. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/ace01ec6-cf68-4ec0-bf28-8e6e6bf008dd.png) In triangle OAC, ∠AOC + ∠OAC + ∠OCA = 180° (sum of angles in a triangle). ∠AOC + 50° + 50° = 180° ∠AOC + 100° = 180° ∠AOC = 180° − 100° ∠AOC = 80° The reflex angle ∠AOC at the center is 360° − ∠AOC = 360° − 80° = 280°. Angle subtended by arc ABC at the center is reflex ∠AOC = 280°. Angle subtended by arc ABC at the circumference is ∠ABC. ∠ABC = (1/2) × reflex ∠AOC = (1/2) × 280° = 140°. Consider triangle OAB. Since OA and OB are radii of the same circle, OA = OB. Therefore, ∠OBA = ∠OAB. In triangle OAB, ∠AOB + ∠OAB + ∠OBA = 180°. 140° + ∠OAB + ∠OAB = 180° 140° + 2∠OAB = 180° 2∠OAB = 180° − 140° 2∠OAB = 40° ∠OAB = 20° ∠BAC = ∠OAC − ∠OAB = 50° − 20° = 30°. ∴ The value of angle ∠BAC is 30°.

questions.question: A cone of height 12 cm is sliced by two planes parallel to the base at heights of 4 cm and 8 cm from the vertex. Find the ratio of the volumes of the three resulting sections (top:middle:bottom).
questions.explanation: Given Total height of cone = 12 cm Slices from vertex at heights 4 cm and 8 cm Formula Used ​V1V2=(h1h2)3\frac{V_1}{V_2} = \left(\frac{h_1}{h_2}\right)^3V2​V1​​=(h2​h1​​)3​​ Solution Heights of the three similar cones formed from the top: h1h_1 h1​ = 4 cm, h2h_2h2​​ = 8 cm, h3h_3h3​​ = 12 cm Ratio of these heights: 4 : 8 : 12 = 1 : 2 : 3 Cube the height ratio to find the ratio of their volumes: 13:23:331^3 : 2^3 : 3^3 13:23:33​= 1 : 8 : 27 Top section volume = 1 unit. Middle section volume = 8 - 1 = 7 units. Bottom section volume = 27 - 8 = 19 units. Final ratio = 1 : 7 : 19. Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: The area of a quadrilateral whose vertices are (3, 0), (4, 5), (-1, 4) and (-2,-1) taken in order, is:
Two numbers are in the ratio 6 : 7 and if 16 is added to the first number and 16 is subtracted from the second number then, the ratio becomes 2 : 1. What is the difference between these two numbers?
questions.explanation: ​Given: Initial Ratio=6:7 Operation: Add 16 to first, Subtract 16 from second. New Ratio=2:1 Solution: Let the numbers be 6x and 7x. 6x+167x−16=21 1(6x+16)=2(7x−16) 6x+16=14x−32 14x−6x=16+32 8x=48 x=6 Difference=7x−6x=x=6 Final Answer 6\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Initial Ratio} = 6 : 7 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Operation: Add 16 to first, Subtract 16 from second.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{New Ratio} = 2 : 1 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Let the numbers be } 6x \text{ and } 7x. \\ \space \, \\ \frac{6x + 16}{7x - 16} = \frac{2}{1} \\ \space \, \\ 1(6x + 16) = 2(7x - 16) \\ \space \, \\ 6x + 16 = 14x - 32 \\ \space \, \\ 14x - 6x = 16 + 32 \\ \space \, \\ 8x = 48 \\ \space \, \\ x = 6 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Difference} = 7x - 6x = x = 6 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ 6Given: Initial Ratio=6:7 Operation: Add 16 to first, Subtract 16 from second. New Ratio=2:1 Solution: Let the numbers be 6x and 7x. 7x−166x+16​=12​ 1(6x+16)=2(7x−16) 6x+16=14x−32 14x−6x=16+32 8x=48 x=6 Difference=7x−6x=x=6 Final Answer 6​​

questions.question: A company increases the dimensions of a product box each day. On the first day, the box length is 2.1 cm, and each day, the length increases by 0.4 cm. What will be the length of the box on the 8th day?
questions.explanation: ​Given: First term (a)=2.1 Common difference (d)=0.4 Number of terms (n)=8 Formula Used: Nth term of an AP: Tn=a+(n−1)d Solution: T8=2.1+(8−1)(0.4) T8=2.1+(7)(0.4) T8=2.1+2.8=4.9 Final Answer 4.9 cm\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{First term } (a) = 2.1 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Common difference } (d) = 0.4 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Number of terms } (n) = 8 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Formula Used:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Nth term of an AP: } T_n = a + (n-1)d \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ T_8 = 2.1 + (8 - 1)(0.4) \\ \space \, \\ T_8 = 2.1 + (7)(0.4) \\ \space \, \\ T_8 = 2.1 + 2.8 = 4.9 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ 4.9 \text{ cm}Given: First term (a)=2.1 Common difference (d)=0.4 Number of terms (n)=8 Formula Used: Nth term of an AP: Tn​=a+(n−1)d Solution: T8​=2.1+(8−1)(0.4) T8​=2.1+(7)(0.4) T8​=2.1+2.8=4.9 Final Answer 4.9 cm​​

questions.question: Identify the incorrect relationship(s) from the list below: i √7 + √3 = √6 + √4 ii √7 + √3 < √6 + √4 iii √7 + √3 > √6 + √4
questions.explanation: Given: (i) √7 + √3 = √6 + √4 (ii) √7 + √3 < √6 + √4 (iii) √7 + √3 > √6 + √4 Concept Used: Comparison of surds by squaring both sides (since all values are positive). Formula Used: (a + b)² = a² + b² + 2ab Solution: First, compare the two expressions. (√7 + √3)² = 7 + 3 + 2√21 = 10 + 2√21 (√6 + √4)² = 6 + 4 + 2√24 = 10 + 4√6 Now compare 2√21 and 4√6. (2√21)² = 84 (4√6)² = 96 Since 84 < 96 => 2√21 < 4√6 => √7 + √3 < √6 + √4 Now checking each statement separately: (i) √7 + √3 = √6 + √4 This is incorrect because the left side is smaller, not equal. (ii) √7 + √3 < √6 + √4 This is correct because we proved the left side is smaller. (iii) √7 + √3 > √6 + √4 This is incorrect because the left side is not greater. Thus, the incorrect relationships are (i) and (iii).

questions.question: A bookseller purchases 10 notebooks at the marked price of 8 notebooks from a distributor. If he then sells these notebooks to customers, offering a discount of 10% on the marked price, what is his profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Given Cost Price of 10 notebooks = Marked Price of 8 notebooks Discount offered = 10% on Marked Price Formula Used ​Profit %=Selling Price−Cost PriceCost Price×100 \text{Profit \%} = \frac{\text{Selling Price} - \text{Cost Price}}{\text{Cost Price}} \times 100Profit %=Cost PriceSelling Price−Cost Price​×100​​ Solution Let the Marked Price (MP) of 1 notebook be 10 Then, MP of 10 notebooks = 100 Cost Price (CP) of 10 notebooks = MP of 8 notebooks = 8×108 \times 10 8×10​= 80 Selling Price (SP) of 1 notebook after 10% discount = 10 - (10×1010010 \times \frac{10}{100}10×10010​​) = 9 SP of 10 notebooks = 10 ×\times×​ 9 = 90 Profit on 10 notebooks = 90 - 80 = 10 Profit Percentage = 1080×100=1008=12.5% \frac{10}{80} \times 100 = \frac{100}{8} = 12.5\%8010​×100=8100​=12.5%​​ Final Answer So the correct answer is (d) ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/4aca5479-25ed-40ae-829e-8884f17bb1da.png)

questions.question: Aryan has to reach Ahmedabad which is 889 km away in 17 hours. His starting speed for 5 hours was 29 km/hr. For the next 96 km his speed was 24 km/hr. By what speed he must travel now so as to reach Ahmedabad in decided time of 17 hours?
questions.explanation: Given: Total distance to Ahmedabad = 889 km Total time available = 17 hours First part: 5 hours at 29 km/hr Second part: 96 km at 24 km/hr Required: Speed for remaining distance Formula Used: Distance = Speed × Time Solution: Distance covered in first 5 hours = 29 × 5 = 145 km Distance covered next = 96 km Time taken = 9624\frac{96}{ 24}2496​​ = 4 hours Total distance covered so far = 145 + 96 = 241 km Remaining distance = 889 − 241 = 648 km Time consumed so far = 5 + 4 = 9 hours Time left = 17 − 9 = 8 hours Required speed = 6488\frac{648} 8 8648​​= 81 km/hr

questions.question: In ∆ABC, DE||BC, intersecting AB at D and AC at E. AD : BD :: 2 : 3 and the area of trapezium BDEC is 63 cm2. What is the area of ∆ADE?
questions.explanation: Given: In ΔABC, DE || BC, intersecting AB at D and AC at E AD : BD :: 2 : 3 The area of trapezium BDEC is 63 cm² Formula used: Area of Trapezium = 1/2 × (Sum of parallel sides) × Height Concept used: Similarity Property = AD : AB = DE : BC = AE : AC ΔADE/ΔABC = (AD)² / (AB)² Solution: According to the question, ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/a05302aa-194f-48f9-8fc2-2892f2172c58.png) In ΔABC, =&gt DE || BC =&gt ∠A is common =&gt ∠D = ∠B and ∠E = ∠C [Angle made by parallel lines] Hence, ΔADE is congruent to ΔABC Now we have, =&gt AD/BD = 2/3 =&gt AD/AB = 2/5 =&gt ΔADE/ΔABC = (AD)² / (AB)² = 4/25 Now Area of ΔADE Area of ΔADE = (Area of ΔADE) - (Area of trapezium BDEC) =&gt Area of ΔADE = 25/4(Area of ΔADE) - 63 =&gt 63 = ar(ΔADE)(25/4 - 1) =&gt 63 = ar(ΔADE)(21/4) =&gt ar(ΔADE) = 4/21 × 63 = 12 cm​2

questions.question: A shopkeeper buys books for ₹300 each. He marks them at ₹450. He offers a "Buy 2, Get 1 Free" deal. What is his profit percentage per book sold effectively?
questions.explanation: Given: CP per book = ₹300 MP per book = ₹450 Offer: Buy 2, Get 1 Free Solution: For a customer buying 2 books, they get 3 books total. Customer pays for 2 books at MP: SP (Total) = 2 × 450 = 900 Shopkeeper sells 3 books. CP (Total) = 3 × 300 = 900 Profit = SP - CP = 900 - 900 = 0 Profit % = 0% Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: A can finish a task in 12 days, and B can finish the same task in 16 days. They work on alternate days starting with A, however, on every fifth day, C steps in and works for that day alone and so on. C can finish the task in 18 days. How many total days will it take to complete the task?
questions.explanation: Given Time A = 12 days Time B = 16 days Time C = 18 days Pattern: A, B, A, B, C (repeats) Solution Total work = LCM(12, 16, 18) = 144 units. Efficiencies: A = 14412=12 \frac{144}{12} = 1212144​=12​ units/day B = 14416=9 \frac{144}{16} = 916144​=9​ units/day C = 14418\frac{144}{18}18144​​ = 8 units/day Work done in one cycle (5 days): Day 1 (A): 12 Day 2 (B): 9 Day 3 (A): 12 Day 4 (B): 9 Day 5 (C): 8 Total in 5 days = 50 units. Target is 144 units. 2 cycles = 50× \times×​ 2 = 100 units in 5 × \times×​ 2 = 10 days. Remaining work = 144 - 100 = 44 units. Proceed day by day: Day 11 (A): 12 units. Rem = 32. Day 12 (B): 9 units. Rem = 23. Day 13 (A): 12 units. Rem = 11. Day 14 (B): 9 units. Rem = 2. Day 15 (C): Needs to do 2 units. Time taken by C =28=14 \frac{2}{8} = \frac{1}{4}82​=41​​day. Total Time = 14 + 14=1414\frac{1}{4} = 14 \frac{1}{4} 41​=1441​​days. Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: How many different pairs (x,y) can be formed using the numbers {1,2,3,…,43} such that x<y and the sum of x and y is even?
questions.explanation: Given: Set S={1,2,3,,43}. We want the number of pairs (x,y) such that x<y and x+y is even. Formula Used: x+y is even if both x and y are odd or both are even. ​Total pairs where x<y = (n2) = n(n−1)2.\text{Total pairs where } x<y \;=\; \binom{n}{2} \;=\; \frac{n(n-1)}{2}.Total pairs where x<y=(2n​)=2n(n−1)​.​​ Solution: From 1 to 43: there are 22 odd numbers (1,3.........43) and 21 even numbers (2,4,............42). Pairs from odd numbers =22×212=231 Pairs from even numbers =21×202=210\text{Pairs from odd numbers }= \frac{22 \times 21}{2} = 231\\\ \\\text{Pairs from even numbers }= \frac{21 \times 20}{2} = 210Pairs from odd numbers =222×21​=231 Pairs from even numbers =221×20​=210​​ Total valid pairs with x<y and even sum: 231 + 210 = 441.

questions.question: A solid metallic sphere is cut into 8 identical pieces by making 3 mutually perpendicular cuts through its centre. By what percentage is the sum of the total surface areas of the 8 pieces more than the surface area of the original sphere?
questions.explanation: Given Solid sphere cut by 3 mutually perpendicular cuts. Number of pieces = 8. Formula Used Surface Area of Sphere = 4πr24\pi r^24πr2​​ Area of a circle = πr2\pi r^2πr2​​ Solution Original Surface Area = 4πr24\pi r^24πr2​​ Each cut through the center creates 2 new circular faces. Total new faces from 3 cuts = 3×2=6faces.3 \times 2 = 6 faces.3×2=6faces.​​ Area of 6 faces = 6πr2 6\pi r^26πr2​​ Total Surface Area of 8 pieces = Original Area + Area of new faces Total Area = 4πr2+6πr2=10πr2 4\pi r^2 + 6\pi r^2 = 10\pi r^24πr2+6πr2=10πr2​​ Increase in Area = 10πr2−4πr2=6πr210\pi r^2 - 4\pi r^2 = 6\pi r^210πr2−4πr2=6πr2​​ Percentage Increase = 6πr24πr2×100%=1.5×100%=150% \frac{6\pi r^2}{4\pi r^2} \times 100\%= 1.5 \times 100\% = 150\%4πr26πr2​×100%=1.5×100%=150%​​ Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: The average marks in English scored by the students of a class in an exam were 46. If 4 of these students who actually scored 15, 13, 9 and 11 are not counted, the average marks for the class in the exam would have been 54. Find the number of students in the class.
questions.explanation: Given Initial Average = 46 Marks of 4 removed students: 15, 13, 9, 11 New Average = 54 Formula Used Sum = Average × Number of Students Solution Let total students be N. Sum of marks = 46N Sum of marks of 4 students removed = 15 + 13 + 9 + 11 = 48 Remaining students =N - 4 New sum = 46N - 48 According to the question: ​46N−48N−4=54\frac{46N - 48}{N - 4} = 54N−446N−48​=54​​ 46N - 48 = 54(N - 4) 46N - 48 = 54N - 216 216 - 48 = 54N - 46N 168 = 8N N = 21 Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Study the following pie-chart and answer the question given below. Breakup (degree wise) of the number of students studying in classes VI to X in a school ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/989612fd-930f-43a4-afe1-2830d1e18e20.png) Number of students in Class VIII = 155 In Class IX, the ratio of boys and girls is 2 : 3 and 5559\frac 5995​​ % of the students in Class X are girls. The numberof girls in Class X is what percent more than the number of boys in Class IX?
questions.explanation: ​Given : ​From the pie-chart (degree wise): Class VI = 84.6° Class VII = 75.6° Class VIII = 63° Class IX = 72° Class X = 64.8° Number of students in Class VIII = 155 In Class IX, boys : girls = 2 : 3 In Class X, 5559%=59\dfrac{5}{9}\% = \dfrac{5}{9}95​%=95​​ are girls Formula Used : ​Students=Degree360∘×Total students\text{Students} = \frac{\text{Degree}}{360^\circ} \times \text{Total students}Students=360∘Degree​×Total students​​ ​Percentage more=DifferenceBase value×100\text{Percentage more} = \frac{\text{Difference}}{\text{Base value}} \times 100Percentage more=Base valueDifference​×100​​ Solution : Find total number of students ​63∘→155 students63^\circ \rightarrow 155 \text{ students}63∘→155 students​​ ​360∘→155×36063360^\circ \rightarrow \frac{155 \times 360}{63}360∘→63155×360​​​ = 885.7≈886 students5.7 \approx 886 \text{ students}5.7≈886 students​​ Class IX students ​=72360×886≈177= \frac{72}{360} \times 886 \approx 177=36072​×886≈177​​ Class X students ​=64.8360×886≈160= \frac{64.8}{360} \times 886 \approx 160=36064.8​×886≈160​​ Boys : Girls = 2 : 3 Total parts = 5 ​Boys in IX=25×177≈71\text{Boys in IX} = \frac{2}{5} \times 177 \approx 71Boys in IX=52​×177≈71​​ ​Girls in X=59×160≈89\text{Girls in X} = \frac{5}{9} \times 160 \approx 89Girls in X=95​×160≈89​​ Difference: = 89 - 71 = 18 ​Required %=1871×100≈25\text{Required \%} = \frac{18}{71} \times 100 \approx 25Required %=7118​×100≈25​%

questions.question: For a regular polygon, the sum of the interior angles is 400% more than the sum of its exterior angles. Each interior angle of the polygon measures x°. What is the value of x?​
questions.explanation: ​Given:Sum of interior angles is 400% more than sum of exterior anglesEach interior angle=x∘Concept Used:Interior and exterior angles of a regular polygonFormula Used:Sum of exterior angles=360∘Sum of interior angles=(n−2)×180∘Each interior angle=(n−2)180∘nSolution:400% more than 360∘=360∘+4(360∘)=1800∘(n−2)180=1800n−2=10n=12x=(12−2)18012=180012=150∘Final Answer:150∘\\\textbf{Given:}\\\text{Sum of interior angles is } 400\% \text{ more than sum of exterior angles}\\\text{Each interior angle} = x^\circ\\\\\textbf{Concept Used:}\\\text{Interior and exterior angles of a regular polygon}\\\\\textbf{Formula Used:}\\\text{Sum of exterior angles} = 360^\circ\\\text{Sum of interior angles} = (n-2)\times 180^\circ\\\text{Each interior angle} = \frac{(n-2)180^\circ}{n}\\\\\textbf{Solution:}\\400\% \text{ more than } 360^\circ = 360^\circ + 4(360^\circ) = 1800^\circ\\\\(n-2)180 = 1800\\n-2 = 10\\n = 12\\\\x = \frac{(12-2)180}{12}\\= \frac{1800}{12}\\= 150^\circ\\\\\textbf{Final Answer:}\\150^\circ\\Given:Sum of interior angles is 400% more than sum of exterior anglesEach interior angle=x∘Concept Used:Interior and exterior angles of a regular polygonFormula Used:Sum of exterior angles=360∘Sum of interior angles=(n−2)×180∘Each interior angle=n(n−2)180∘​Solution:400% more than 360∘=360∘+4(360∘)=1800∘(n−2)180=1800n−2=10n=12x=12(12−2)180​=121800​=150∘Final Answer:150∘​​

questions.question: The sum of squares of all the roots of the equation: ​(x+1x)2−4=32(x−1x),x≠0\left(x + \frac{1}{x}\right)^2 - 4 = \frac{3}{2} \left(x - \frac{1}{x}\right), x \neq 0(x+x1​)2−4=23​(x−x1​),x=0 is:
questions.explanation: ​Given:(x+1x)2−4=32(x−1x), x≠0Concept Used:Algebraic identities and polynomial equationsFormula Used:(x+1x)2=x2+1x2+2Solution:x2+1x2−2=32(x−1x)2x2+2x2−4=3x−3x2x4−4x2+2=3x3−3x2x4−3x3−4x2+3x+2=0(x−1)(x+1)(2x2−3x−2)=0x=1, −1, −12, 2Sum of squares=1+1+14+4=254=614\textbf{Given:} \\\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^2 - 4 = \frac{3}{2}\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right),\; x \neq 0 \\\textbf{Concept Used:} \\\text{Algebraic identities and polynomial equations} \\\textbf{Formula Used:} \\\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^2 = x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2} + 2 \\\textbf{Solution:} \\x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2} - 2 = \frac{3}{2}\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right) \\2x^2 + \frac{2}{x^2} - 4 = 3x - \frac{3}{x} \\2x^4 - 4x^2 + 2 = 3x^3 - 3x \\2x^4 - 3x^3 - 4x^2 + 3x + 2 = 0 \\(x-1)(x+1)(2x^2-3x-2)=0 \\x = 1,\,-1,\,-\frac{1}{2},\,2 \\\text{Sum of squares} = 1 + 1 + \frac{1}{4} + 4 = \frac{25}{4} =6\frac 14Given:(x+x1​)2−4=23​(x−x1​),x=0Concept Used:Algebraic identities and polynomial equationsFormula Used:(x+x1​)2=x2+x21​+2Solution:x2+x21​−2=23​(x−x1​)2x2+x22​−4=3x−x3​2x4−4x2+2=3x3−3x2x4−3x3−4x2+3x+2=0(x−1)(x+1)(2x2−3x−2)=0x=1,−1,−21​,2Sum of squares=1+1+41​+4=425​=641​​​

questions.question: If A (12, 21), B (3, p) and C (9, 0) are the vertices of a right-angled triangle with AC hypotenuse, then p = _________.
questions.explanation: Given: ABC if a right-angled triangle with AC hypotenuse Point of A = 12, 21 Point of B = 3, p Point of C = 9, 0 Formula Used: Distance=(x2−x1)2−(y2−y1)2\text{Distance} = \sqrt{(x_2 - x_1)^2 - (y_2 - y_1)^2}Distance=(x2​−x1​)2−(y2​−y1​)2​ Solution: As we know, ABC is a right-angled triangle with AC hypotenuse So, AC² = AB² + BC² …..(1) For Distance of AB is x₂ = 3, x₁ = 12, y₂ = p, y₁ = 21 Distance of AB = √((x₂ − x₁)² + (y₂ − y₁)²) = √((3 − 12)² + (p − 21)²) = √(9² + p² + 441 − 42p) = √(81 + p² + 441 − 42p) = √(p² + 522 − 42p) AB² = p² + 552 − 42p For Distance of AC is x₂ = 9, x₁ = 12, y₂ = 0, y₁ = 21 Distance of AC = √((x₂ − x₁)² + (y₂ − y₁)²) = √((9 − 12)² + (0 − 21)²) = √(3² + (−21)²) = √(9 + 441) = √450 AC² = 450 For Distance of BC is x₂ = 9, x₁ = 3, y₂ = 0, y₁ = p Distance of BC = √((x₂ − x₁)² + (y₂ − y₁)²) = √((9 − 3)² + (0 − p)²) = √(6² + (−p)²) = √36 + p² BC² = 36 + p² Now put the value in eq(1) 450 = 36 + p² + p² + 522 − 42p 2p² − 42p − 108 = 0 p² − 21p − 54 = 0 p(p − 3) − 18(p − 3) = 0 (p − 3)(p − 18) = 0 p = 18, 3 So, the correct option is (c)

questions.question: If tan⁡θ+cot⁡θ\tan \theta + \cot \theta tanθ+cotθ​= 5 and tan⁡θ⋅cot⁡θ\tan \theta \cdot \cot \thetatanθ⋅cotθ​ = 1, find tan⁡2θ+cosec⁡2θ.\tan^2 \theta + \cosec^2 \theta.tan2θ+cosec2θ.​​
questions.explanation: Given: ​tan⁡θ+cot⁡θ=5tan⁡θ⋅cot⁡θ=1\tan \theta + \cot \theta = 5\\\tan \theta \cdot \cot \theta = 1\\tanθ+cotθ=5tanθ⋅cotθ=1​​ Formula Used: ​(a+b)2=a2+b2+2ab 1+cot⁡2θ=csc⁡2θ(a + b)^2 = a^2 + b^2 + 2ab\\ \ \\1 + \cot^2 \theta = \csc^2 \theta\\(a+b)2=a2+b2+2ab 1+cot2θ=csc2θ​​ Solution: ​(tan⁡θ+cot⁡θ)2=52 tan⁡2θ+cot⁡2θ+2tan⁡θcot⁡θ=25 (\tan \theta + \cot \theta)^2 = 5^2\\ \ \\\tan^2 \theta + \cot^2 \theta + 2\tan \theta \cot \theta = 25\\ \ \\(tanθ+cotθ)2=52 tan2θ+cot2θ+2tanθcotθ=25 Substitute the product value ​tan⁡2θ+cot⁡2θ+2(1)=25 tan⁡2θ+cot⁡2θ=23\tan^2 \theta + \cot^2 \theta + 2(1) = 25\\ \ \\\tan^2 \theta + \cot^2 \theta = 23\\tan2θ+cot2θ+2(1)=25 tan2θ+cot2θ=23​​ Assuming the expression is: tan⁡2θ+cosec⁡2θ \tan^2 \theta + \cosec^2 \theta\\tan2θ+cosec2θ​​ ​=tan⁡2θ+(1+cot⁡2θ)=(tan⁡2θ+cot⁡2θ)+1=23+1=24= \tan^2 \theta + (1 + \cot^2 \theta)\\= (\tan^2 \theta + \cot^2 \theta) + 1\\= 23 + 1 = 24\\=tan2θ+(1+cot2θ)=(tan2θ+cot2θ)+1=23+1=24​​ So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Two numbers 4562 and 4634 are divided by a greatest two-digit number and leave the same remainder. What is the sum of the digits of the divisor?
questions.explanation: Given: Numbers: 4562 and 4634 They leave the same remainder when divided by a 2-digit number. Formula Used: If two numbers leave the same remainder when divided by n, then n must be a factor of their difference. Solution: Difference = 4634 - 4562 = 72 Factors of 72: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 9, 12, 18, 24, 36, 72. Two-digit factors are: 12, 18, 24, 36, 72. We need to find the sum of digits of the divisor. Possible sums: 12: 1+2=3 18: 1+8=9 24: 2+4=6 36: 3+6=9 72: 7+2=9 From the options, 9 is the correct match (Option B). Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: What is the mode of the data given below? [Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.] Age in years 15-25 25-35 35-45 45-55 55-65 65-75 75-85 No. of patients 31 34 13 19 30 28 29
questions.explanation: ​Given:Modal class=25–35l=25, h=10f1=34, f0=31, f2=13Concept Used:Mode of grouped dataFormula Used:Mode=l+f1−f02f1−f0−f2×hSolution:Mode=25+34−312(34)−31−13×10=25+324×10=25+1.25=26.25Final Answer:26.25\textbf{Given:} \\\\\text{Modal class} = 25\text{–}35 \\\\l = 25,\ h = 10 \\\\f_1 = 34,\ f_0 = 31,\ f_2 = 13 \\\\\textbf{Concept Used:} \\\\\text{Mode of grouped data} \\\\\textbf{Formula Used:} \\\\\text{Mode} = l + \frac{f_1 - f_0}{2f_1 - f_0 - f_2} \times h \\\\\textbf{Solution:} \\\\\text{Mode} = 25 + \frac{34 - 31}{2(34) - 31 - 13} \times 10 \\\\= 25 + \frac{3}{24} \times 10 \\\\= 25 + 1.25 \\\\= 26.25 \\\\\textbf{Final Answer:} \\\\26.25Given:Modal class=25–35l=25, h=10f1​=34, f0​=31, f2​=13Concept Used:Mode of grouped dataFormula Used:Mode=l+2f1​−f0​−f2​f1​−f0​​×hSolution:Mode=25+2(34)−31−1334−31​×10=25+243​×10=25+1.25=26.25Final Answer:26.25​​

questions.question: Given that 950.53=x, 950.83=y95^{0.53} = x,\; 95^{0.83} = y950.53=x,950.83=y​ and xz=y8x^{z} = y^{8}xz=y8​, then the value of z is close to:
questions.explanation: Given: x = 950.5395^{0.53}950.53​​ y = 950.83 95^{0.83}950.83​​ ​xz=y8x^z = y^8xz=y8​​ Solution: Substitute the values of x and y into the equation: ​(950.53)z=(950.83)8(95^{0.53})^z = (95^{0.83})^8(950.53)z=(950.83)8​​ Using the property (am)n=amn:(a^m)^n = a^{mn}:(am)n=amn:​​ ​950.53z=950.83×895^{0.53z} = 95^{0.83 \times 8}950.53z=950.83×8​​ Since the bases are equal, we can equate the exponents: ​0.53z=0.83×80.53z = 0.83 \times 80.53z=0.83×8​​ 0.53z = 6.64 ​z=6.640.53=66453z = \frac{6.64}{0.53} = \frac{664}{53}z=0.536.64​=53664​​​ z≈ \approx≈​ 12.528... The value of z is closest to 12.53 Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)
